title: layout block as separate top-level declaration splits struct/layout definition
description: The layout block is a separate top-level declaration 'layout TypeName { ... }' that references a struct by name. Problems: (1) A struct can exist without a layout — nothing in the struct definition tells you a layout exists elsewhere. (2) If the struct is renamed, the layout silently becomes an orphan or attach error. (3) The struct and its layout can be in different files/modules with no connection visible at the struct definition site. Suggested restructuring: inline the layout block inside the struct definition, as Ada's representation clauses are inline with the type declaration. This makes the struct's binary contract self-contained.
